codeReviewIssues.description: update_clock and update_clock_inline hardcode `advance = 8u16` for monospace char width instead of deriving it from the existing Content::Glyphs data or a config parameter. build_editor_scene uses the `char_width` parameter. Pre-existing behavior (not introduced by this feature), but if char_width ever changes from 8, the clock update path would produce misaligned glyphs.

Tests exercise a build_test_editor_scene helper that mirrors production logic rather than calling SceneState::build_editor_scene directly. This is reasonable (SceneState requires shared memory / DoubleWriter), but means the structural contract tests don't exercise the actual production code path — they verify the pattern is correct. The 15 new tests are thorough for contract verification; direct integration testing would require a different test harness.

Per-line Glyphs content_hash uses fnv1a(&glyph_ref.offset.to_le_bytes()) — hashing the data buffer offset rather than the actual text content. This is fine for damage detection (different offset = different frame position = re-render needed) but unconventional. The N_DOC_TEXT parent already hashes the full doc_text, so this serves as a per-node differentiator.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker noted in skillFeedback.suggestedChanges that the os-worker skill should mention hybrid refactoring features (where most work was done by prior features but some new implementation remains). The skill currently has a binary distinction: 'pure refactoring' (skip new tests) vs 'implementation features' (TDD). This feature was 90% complete from prior work — only cursor/selection FillRect migration remained. The worker correctly wrote verification tests for the whole contract rather than just the new changes, which was the right call but not explicitly guided by the skill.

summary: Clean pass. The commit (951fcf8) makes exactly 3 surgical changes to scene_state.rs (+16 net lines): (1) cursor background → Content::FillRect, (2) selection background → Content::FillRect, (3) update_selection now preserves per-line Glyphs children by counting them before truncating. 15 new tests (693 lines) comprehensively verify the scene graph contract (all VAL-CORE-001 through VAL-CORE-007). Content::Text is completely removed from the entire system/ directory. QEMU visual verification confirmed identical rendering. The critical bug fix in update_selection — which previously truncated ALL dynamic nodes including line Glyphs — was caught and fixed within this feature. No blocking issues found.
